title: I Slept, And Dreamed That Life Was Beauty
content:
"I slept, and dreamed that life was beauty;
        I woke, and found that life was duty.
        Was thy dream then a shadowy lie?
        Toil on, sad heart, courageously,
        And thou shall find thy dream to be
        A noonday light and truth to thee."
meaning:
Louisa May Alcott's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "I Slept, And Dreamed That Life Was Beauty"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
